title: ceratopteris lockhartii
intro: Ceratopteris lockhartii, also known as the water sprite or Australian water fern, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ceratopteris lockhartii include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and it is often used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asian and Pacific Island cultures.
